en.extract:
The Socorro parakeet (Psittacara brevipes), known in aviculture as the Socorro green conure or Socorro conure, is a species of bird in subfamily Arinae of the family Psittacidae, the African and New World parrots. It is endemic to Socorro Island in the Revillagigedo Islands, Mexico.


Taxonomy and systematics
The International Ornithological Committee and the Clements taxonomy treat the Socorro parakeet as a full, monotypic, species. BirdLife International's Handbook of the Birds of the World (HBW) considers it to be a subspecies of the green parakeet (P. holochlorus).


Description
The Socorro parakeet is 31 to 33 cm (12 to 13 in) long. The sexes are alike. It is essentially all green with a somewhat olive tinge to the underside of the flight feathers and tail. Its iris is orange-red surrounded by bare purplish brown skin, its bill horn colored, and its legs brownish.


Distribution and habitat
The Socorro parakeet is found only on Socorro Island off the western coast of Mexico. It inhabits forests dominated by Bumelia socorrensis, Ilex socorroensis, and Guettarda insularis, and is usually found at elevations above 500 m (1,600 ft).


Behavior


Movement
The Socorro parakeet is sedentary.


Feeding
One study found that about half of the Socorro parakeet's diet was seeds and fruit pulp of Bumelia socorrensis with those of Ilex socorrensis, Guettarda insularis, and Psidium making up most of the rest.


Breeding
The Socorro parakeet breeds between October and January. It typically nests in a cavity in a Bumelia socorrensis tree about 2.3 to 3.8 m (8 to 10 ft) above the ground.


Vocalization
As of early 2023, xeno-canto had only three recordings of Socorro parakeet vocalizations; the Cornell Lab of Ornithology's Macaulay Library had many. Neither described the vocalizations in words.


Status
The IUCN follows HBW taxonomy and so has not assessed the Socorro parakeet separately from the green parakeet. It does note that as a subspecies it is "regarded as highly threatened". The species is threatened by habitat destruction by sheep and predation by feral cats. In 2006 and 2007 its population was estimated at about 300 individuals, a decrease from estimates in the early 1990s of 400 to 500. It may meet the criteria for a Vulnerable or Endangered species.

The '''Socorro parakeet''' ('''''Psittacara brevipes'''''), known in [[aviculture]] as the '''Socorro green conure''' or '''Socorro conure'''<ref name="worldparrottrust">{{cite web |title=SOCORRO CONURE  (Psittacara brevipes) |url=https://www.parrots.org/encyclopedia/socorro-conure |publisher=World Parrot Trust |access-date=31 March 2023}}</ref><!-- -->, is a species of bird in subfamily [[Arinae]] of the family [[Psittacidae]], the African and New World parrots. It is [[endemic]] to [[Socorro Island]] in the [[Revillagigedo Islands]], Mexico.<ref name=IOC13.1>{{cite web |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/bow/parrots/ |title=Parrots, cockatoos |website=IOC World Bird List |version =v 13.1 |editor-last1=Gill |editor-first1= F. |editor-last2=Donsker|editor-first2=D.|editor-last3=Rasmussen |editor-first3=P. |date=January 2023 |access-date=18 February 2023 }}</ref>

==Taxonomy and systematics==

The [[International Ornithological Committee]] and the [[Clements taxonomy]] treat the Socorro parakeet as a full, [[monotypic]], species.<ref name=IOC13.1/><ref name=Clements2022>Clements, J. F., T. S. Schulenberg, M. J. Iliff, T. A. Fredericks, J. A. Gerbracht, D. Lepage, S. M. Billerman, B. L. Sullivan, and C. L. Wood. 2022. The eBird/Clements checklist of birds of the world: v2022. Downloaded from https://www.birds.cornell.edu/clementschecklist/download/ retrieved November 10, 2022</ref> [[BirdLife International]]'s ''[[Handbook of the Birds of the World]]'' (HBW) considers it to be a subspecies of the [[green parakeet]] (''P. holochlorus'').<ref name=HBW7/>

==Description==

The Socorro parakeet is {{convert|31|to|33|cm|in|sigfig=2|abbr=on}} long. The sexes are alike. It is essentially all green with a somewhat olive tinge to the underside of the flight feathers and tail. Its iris is orange-red surrounded by bare purplish brown skin, its bill horn colored, and its legs brownish.<ref name=SOPA-BOW/><ref name=GRPA-BOW>Eitniear, J. C., N. Collar, C. J. Sharpe, and P. F. D. Boesman (2020). Green Parakeet (''Psittacara holochlorus''), version 1.0. In Birds of the World (S. M. Billerman, B. K. Keeney, P. G. Rodewald, and T. S. Schulenberg, Editors). Cornell Lab of Ornithology, Ithaca, NY, USA. https://doi.org/10.2173/bow.grnpar.01 retrieved April 5, 2023</ref>

==Distribution and habitat==

The Socorro parakeet is found only on Socorro Island off the western coast of Mexico. It inhabits forests dominated by ''[[Bumelia socorrensis]]'', ''[[Ilex socorroensis]]'', and ''[[Guettarda insularis]]'', and is usually found at elevations above {{convert|500|m|ft|sigfig=2|abbr=on}}.<ref name=SOPA-BOW/>

==Behavior==
===Movement===

The Socorro parakeet is sedentary.<ref name=SOPA-BOW/>

===Feeding===

One study found that about half of the Socorro parakeet's diet was seeds and fruit pulp of ''Bumelia socorrensis'' with those of ''Ilex socorrensis'', ''Guettarda insularis'', and ''[[Psidium]]'' making up most of the rest.<ref name=SOPA-BOW/><ref>Rodríguez-Estrella, R., Mata, E. and Rivera, L. (1992). Ecological notes on the Green Parakeet of Isla Socorro, Mexico. Condor. 94(2): 523–525</ref>

===Breeding===

The Socorro parakeet breeds between October and January. It typically nests in a cavity in a ''Bumelia socorrensis'' tree about {{convert|2.3|to|3.8|m|ft|sigfig=1|abbr=on}} above the ground.<ref name=SOPA-BOW/>

{{birdsong|url=https://xeno-canto.org/species/Psittacara-brevipes |species=Socorro parakeet}}

===Vocalization===

As of early 2023, [[xeno-canto]] had only three recordings of Socorro parakeet vocalizations; the [[Cornell Lab of Ornithology]]'s [[Macaulay Library]] had many. Neither described the vocalizations in words.<ref name=SOPA-BOW/>

==Status==

The [[IUCN]] follows HBW taxonomy and so has not assessed the Socorro parakeet separately from the green parakeet. It does note that as a subspecies it is "regarded as highly threatened".<ref name=IUCN>{{cite iucn |author=BirdLife International |date=2018 |title=Green Parakeet ''Aratinga holochlorus'' |volume=2018 |article-number=e.T62296899A132190435 |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2018-2.RLTS.T62296899A132190435.en |access-date=5 April 2023}}</ref> The species is threatened by [[habitat destruction]] by sheep and predation by feral cats. In 2006 and 2007 its population was estimated at about 300 individuals, a decrease from estimates in the early 1990s of 400 to 500.<ref name=IUCN/><ref name=SOPA-BOW/> It may meet the criteria for a [[Vulnerable species|Vulnerable]] or [[Endangered species (IUCN status)|Endangered]] species.<ref>Martínez-Gómez, J. E., Matías-Ferrer, N., & Escalante-Pliego, P. (2017). Phylogeny and taxonomy of the Socorro parakeet (''Psittacara holochlorus brevipes''): Recent speciation with minor morphological differentiation. Journal of Ornithology, 158(4), 965–978. doi:10.1007/s10336-017-1465-4</ref>
